generatedSentence: In the large northland orchard, a grizzled lumberjack, nearly an adult, found a strange artifact, probably not a cheap knockoff. Its foregone moisture made his calling difficult. His evicted rockabilly uncles, an offshoot of pacific monotheism, would anyway misjudge its purpose. The artifact's vibrant, glowing power ignited attacks, prompting a dangerous news raffle against industrial hijackers using warships. A backhanded guideline from a judgy politician, shouting advice, described a deficit, making the rescue operation incredibly risky and daunting. Police were ineffective, scooffed at. The brave newsboy, with his hammerhead catcher's pluck, wore glowing garments and helmets. He flicked a thin ichthus charm, knowing the ultimate untethering of vessels from the industrial gate. Armed with muskets, the gamer and vendor led the gathering against the lumpy wombat's control. Ultimately, the specific equipment, like a washer and drumsticks, helped untether their godlike cause. A positive justification proved the vendor's honesty, twice mentioned in a proverb on a fishbowl's flyleaf. The veejays celebrated this victory, while a wacky earthworm found a roof nacho.
panelSentences: In the large northland orchard, a grizzled lumberjack, nearly an adult, found a strange artifact, probably not a cheap knockoff. Its foregone moisture made his calling difficult. His evicted rockabilly uncles, an offshoot of pacific monotheism, would anyway misjudge its purpose.

The artifact's vibrant, glowing power ignited attacks, prompting a dangerous news raffle against industrial hijackers using warships. A backhanded guideline from a judgy politician, shouting advice, described a deficit, making the rescue operation incredibly risky and daunting. Police were ineffective, scooffed at.

The brave newsboy, with his hammerhead catcher's pluck, wore glowing garments and helmets. He flicked a thin ichthus charm, knowing the ultimate untethering of vessels from the industrial gate. Armed with muskets, the gamer and vendor led the gathering against the lumpy wombat's control.

Ultimately, the specific equipment, like a washer and drumsticks, helped untether their godlike cause. A positive justification proved the vendor's honesty, twice mentioned in a proverb on a fishbowl's flyleaf. The veejays celebrated this victory, while a wacky earthworm found a roof nacho.
rawPanelSentences: In the large northland orchard, a grizzled lumberjack, nearly an adult, found a strange artifact, probably not a cheap knockoff. Its foregone moisture made his calling difficult. His evicted rockabilly uncles, an offshoot of pacific monotheism, would anyway misjudge its purpose.

The artifact's vibrant, glowing power ignited attacks, prompting a dangerous news raffle against industrial hijackers using warships. A backhanded guideline from a judgy politician, shouting advice, described a deficit, making the rescue operation incredibly risky and daunting. Police were ineffective, scooffed at.

The brave newsboy, with his hammerhead catcher's pluck, wore glowing garments and helmets. He flicked a thin ichthus charm, knowing the ultimate untethering of vessels from the industrial gate. Armed with muskets, the gamer and vendor led the gathering against the lumpy wombat's control.

Ultimately, the specific equipment, like a washer and drumsticks, helped untether their godlike cause. A positive justification proved the vendor's honesty, twice mentioned in a proverb on a fishbowl's flyleaf. The veejays celebrated this victory, while a wacky earthworm found a roof nacho.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: A young, determined newsboy wearing a worn, glowing yellow jacket and an antique catcher's helmet. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Newsboy discovering an ancient, pulsating artifact amidst overgrown futuristic machinery, close-up, low angle. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Spaceships resembling warships engaging in chaotic battle above a city skyline, with shadowy figures resembling hijackers, wide shot, high angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Newsboy, now equipped with a muskets-like device and glowing garments, leading a charge through a futuristic gate against a grotesque, lumpy wombat creature, mid-shot, eye-level. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Newsboy and a diverse group of citizens celebrating their victory, with a futuristic city in the background, a large digital screen displaying positive news, long shot, high angle.

An opinion offered in an effort to be helpful.
definitions.example: She was offered various piece of advice on what to do with her new-found wealth.

definitions.definition: Deliberate consideration; knowledge.
(commonly in plural) Information or news given; intelligence
definitions.example: late advices from France

definitions.definition: In language about financial transactions executed by formal documents, an advisory document.
definitions.example: An advice of an incoming settlement payment order may be given to an off-line receiving bank.

definitions.definition: In commercial language, information communicated by letter; used chiefly in reference to drafts or bills of exchange
definitions.example: a letter of advice

definitions.definition: A communication providing information, such as how an uncertain area of law might apply to possible future actions
definitions.example: An advice issued by a Monitoring Committee could be applicable in a Dutch court

definitions.definition: Counseling to perform a specific legal act.
Counseling to perform a specific illegal act.
In aspect-oriented programming, the code whose execution is triggered when a join point is reached.
To set fire to (something), to light (something)
To spark off (something), to trigger
To commence burning.
To subject to the action of intense heat; to heat strongly; often said of incombustible or infusible substances.
definitions.example: to ignite iron or platinum

definitions.definition: Mischievous; of or befitting an imp.

A kind of firearm formerly carried by the infantry of an army, originally fired by means of a match, or matchlock, for which several mechanical appliances (including the flintlock, and finally the percussion lock) were successively substituted; ultimately superseded by the rifle.
definitions.example: Sam, Sam, pick up thy musket.

definitions.definition: A male Eurasian sparrowhawk.
A protective head covering, usually part of armour.
That which resembles a helmet in form, position, etc.
Deficiency in amount or quality; a falling short; lack.
A situation wherein, or amount whereby, spending exceeds government revenue.
Indicating the last item.
definitions.example: Firstly,… Secondly,… Ultimately,…

definitions.definition: Indicating the most important action.
synonyms: at the end of the day
when all is said and done
definitions.example: Ultimately, he will have to make a decision before the end of the week.

definitions.definition: Used to indicate the etymon at which a given etymological derivation terminates.
definitions.example: Cognate to Gottscheerish boßər, bàsser. Ultimately cognate to standard High German Wasser.

synonyms: at the end of the day
when all is said and done

definitions.definition: To undo by removing a tether.
A person or a company that vends or sells.
A vending machine.
To bundle third-party dependencies with the source code for one's own program.
definitions.example: I distributed my application with a vendored copy of Perl so that it wouldn't use the system copies of Perl where it is installed.

definitions.definition: As the software vendor, to bundle one's own, possibly modified version of dependencies with a standard program.
definitions.example: Strawberry Perl contains vendored copies of some CPAN modules, designed to allow them to run on Windows.

definitions.definition: Someone who presents a television programme of videos; a video jockey.
Vagina, vulva.
Someone or something that catches.
The player that squats behind home plate and receives the pitches from the pitcher
The bottom partner in a homosexual relationship or sexual encounter between two men.
A reason, explanation, or excuse which provides convincing, morally acceptable support for behavior or for a belief or occurrence.
The alignment of text to the left margin (left justification), the right margin (right justification), or both margins (full justification).
Inclined to make judgments; judgmental.
A civil force granted the legal authority for law enforcement and maintaining public order.
definitions.example: Call the police!

definitions.definition: A police officer.
People who seek to enforce norms or standards.
definitions.example: thought police

definitions.definition: The duty of cleaning up.
Policy.
Communal living; civilization.
The regulation of a given community or society; administration, law and order etc.
To enforce the law and keep order among (a group).
definitions.example: Extra security was hired to police the crowd at the big game.

definitions.definition: To clean up an area.
To enforce norms or standards upon.
definitions.example: to police a person's identity

definitions.definition: A thing capable of being affirmed; something real or actual.
A favourable point or characteristic.
Something having a positive value in physics, such as an electric charge.
(grammar) A degree of comparison of adjectives and adverbs.
(grammar) An adjective or adverb in the positive degree.
A positive image; one that displays true colors and shades, as opposed to a negative.
The positive plate of a voltaic or electrolytic cell.
A positive result of a test.
Not negative or neutral.
Formally laid down.
Stated definitively and without qualification.
Fully assured in opinion.
definitions.example: I’m absolutely positive you've spelt that wrong.

definitions.definition: Of number, greater than zero.
Characterized by constructiveness or influence for the better.
Overconfident, dogmatic.
Actual, real, concrete, not theoretical or speculative.
Having more protons than electrons.
(grammar) Describing the primary sense of an adjective, adverb or noun; not comparative, superlative, augmentative nor diminutive.
definitions.example: ‘Better’ is an irregular comparative of the positive form ‘good’.

definitions.definition: Derived from an object by itself; not dependent on changing circumstances or relations; absolute.
definitions.example: The idea of beauty is not positive, but depends on the different tastes of individuals.

definitions.definition: Characterized by the existence or presence of distinguishing qualities or features, rather than by their absence.
definitions.example: The box was not empty – I felt some positive substance within it.

definitions.definition: Characterized by the presence of features which support a hypothesis.
definitions.example: The results of our experiment are positive.

definitions.definition: Of a visual image, true to the original in light, shade and colour values.
definitions.example: A positive photograph can be developed from a photographic negative.

definitions.definition: Favorable, desirable by those interested or invested in that which is being judged.
definitions.example: The first-night reviews were largely positive.

definitions.definition: Wholly what is expressed; colloquially downright, entire, outright.
definitions.example: Good lord, you've built up a positive arsenal of weaponry here.

definitions.definition: Optimistic.
definitions.example: He has a positive outlook on life.

definitions.definition: Electropositive
Basic; metallic; not acid; opposed to negative, and said of metals, bases, and basic radicals.
HIV positive.
(New Age jargon) Good, desirable, healthful, pleasant, enjoyable; (often precedes 'energy', 'thought', 'feeling' or 'emotion').
Having pockmarks
Pitted, or scarred with holes
Incomplete, lacking, having holes
To move or hit (something) with a short, quick motion.
definitions.example: flick one's hair

definitions.definition: To jeer; to laugh with contempt and derision.
To mock; to treat with scorn.
To eat food quickly.
To eat.
A distinguishing attribute or quality.
A remedy for a specific disease or condition.
Specification
(in the plural) The details; particulars.
Explicit or definite
Pertaining to a species.
Pertaining to a taxon at the rank of species
Special, distinctive or unique
Intended for, or applying to, a particular thing
Serving to identify a particular thing (often a disease or condition), with little risk of mistaking something else for it.
definitions.example: a highly specific test, specific and nonspecific symptoms

definitions.definition: Being a remedy for a particular disease
definitions.example: Quinine is a specific medicine in cases of malaria.

definitions.definition: Limited to a particular antibody or antigen
Of a value divided by mass (e.g. specific orbital energy)
Similarly referring to a value divided by any measure which acts to standardize it (e.g. thrust specific fuel consumption, referring to fuel consumption divided by thrust)
A measure compared with a standard reference value by division, to produce a ratio without unit or dimension (e.g. specific refractive index is a pure number, and is relative to that of air)
The act of equipping, or the state of being equipped, as for a voyage or expedition.
Whatever is used in equipping something or someone, for example things needed for an expedition or voyage.
Any of several herbivorous, burrowing marsupials, of the family Vombatidae, mainly found in southern and eastern Australia.
A person who plays any kind of game.
A person whose hobby is video games.
A person who games the system.
(obsolete) A gambler
A doorlike structure outside a house.
Doorway, opening, or passage in a fence or wall.
Movable barrier.
definitions.example: The gate in front of the railroad crossing went up after the train had passed.

definitions.definition: A logical pathway made up of switches which turn on or off. Examples are and, or, nand, etc.
The gap between a batsman's bat and pad.
definitions.example: Singh was bowled through the gate, a very disappointing way for a world-class batsman to get out.

definitions.definition: The amount of money made by selling tickets to a concert or a sports event.
(flow cytometry) A line that separates particle type-clusters on two-dimensional dot plots.
Passageway (as in an air terminal) where passengers can embark or disembark.
The controlling terminal of a field effect transistor (FET).
In a lock tumbler, the opening for the stump of the bolt to pass through or into.
The channel or opening through which metal is poured into the mould; the ingate.
The waste piece of metal cast in the opening; a sprue or sullage piece. Also written geat and git.
A mechanism, in a film camera and projector, that holds each frame momentarily stationary behind the aperture.
A tally mark consisting of four vertical bars crossed by a diagonal, representing a count of five.
To keep something inside by means of a closed gate.
To punish, especially a child or teenager, by not allowing them to go out.
To open a closed ion channel.
To furnish with a gate.
To turn (an image intensifier) on and off selectively as needed, or to avoid damage. See autogating.
A meeting or get-together; a party or social function.
definitions.example: I met her at a gathering of engineers and scientists.

definitions.definition: A group of people or things.
definitions.example: A gathering of fruit.

definitions.definition: A section, a group of bifolios, or sheets of paper, stacked together and folded in half.
definitions.example: This gathering machine forms the backbone of a bookbinding operation.

definitions.definition: A charitable contribution; a collection.
A tumor or boil suppurated or maturated; an abscess.
Of or pertaining to a god
definitions.example: her godly powers = her superhuman powers

definitions.definition: Devoted to a god or God; devout; righteous.
Gloriously good.
In a godly manner; piously; devoutly; righteously.
definitions.example: All that will live godly in Christ Jesus shall suffer persecution. — 2. Tim. iii. 12.

definitions.definition: To give off light from heat or to emit light as if heated.
definitions.example: The fire was still glowing after ten hours.

definitions.definition: To radiate some emotional quality like light.
definitions.example: The zealots glowed with religious fervor.

definitions.definition: To gaze especially passionately at something.
To radiate thermal heat.
definitions.example: After their workout, the gymnasts' faces were glowing red.

definitions.definition: To shine brightly and steadily.
definitions.example: The new baby's room glows with bright, loving colors.

definitions.definition: To make hot; to flush.
To feel hot; to have a burning sensation, as of the skin, from friction, exercise, etc.; to burn.
The action of the verb glow.
That glows or glow.
definitions.example: glowing embers

definitions.definition: Full of praise.
definitions.example: He received glowing references from his previous employers.

definitions.definition: A single item of clothing.
Short for temple garment.
To become larger, to increase in magnitude.
definitions.example: Children grow quickly.

definitions.definition: To appear or sprout.
definitions.example: A long tail began to grow from his backside.

definitions.definition: To develop, to mature.
definitions.example: As I grew throughout adolescence, I came to appreciate many things about human nature.

definitions.definition: To cause or allow something to become bigger, especially to cultivate plants.
definitions.example: Have you ever grown your hair before?

definitions.definition: To assume a condition or quality over time.
definitions.example: The boy grew wise as he matured.

definitions.definition: To become attached or fixed; to adhere.
(19th-mid 20th century) An employee in industry.
An enterprise producing tangible goods or providing certain services to industrial companies.
A bond or stock issued by such a company.
A film made for use within an industry, not for a movie-going audience.
Industrial music.
definitions.example: I wish they'd play more industrial in this club.

definitions.definition: An industrial piercing.
Of or relating to industry, notably manufacturing.
definitions.example: The industrial segment of the economy has seen troubles lately.

definitions.definition: Produced by such industry.
definitions.example: Handicraft is less standardized then industrial products, hence less artistic or rather flawless.

definitions.definition: Used by such industry.
Suitable for use in such industry; industrial-grade.
definitions.example: This is an industrial product—it's much too strong for home use.

definitions.definition: Massive in scale or quantity.
Employed as manpower by such industry.
(of a society or country) Having many industries; industrialized.
definitions.example: Italy is a part-industrial, part-rural nation.

definitions.definition: Belonging or pertaining to the genre of industrial music.
definitions.example: a track with clashing industrial beats

definitions.definition: A stylized representation of a fish, used as a Christian symbol.
A musical conductor's downbeat (because of its similar appearance).
A loss or tearing of paper from the back of a stamp, although not sufficient to create a complete hole.
Any food produced or served in thin slices.
definitions.example: chocolate mint thins

definitions.definition: To make thin or thinner.
To become thin or thinner.
definitions.example: The crowds thinned after the procession had passed: there was nothing more to see.

definitions.definition: To dilute.
To remove some plants or parts of plants in order to improve the growth of what remains.
Having little thickness or extent from one surface to its opposite.
definitions.example: thin plate of metal;  thin paper;  thin board;  thin covering

definitions.definition: Very narrow in all diameters; having a cross section that is small in all directions.
definitions.example: thin wire;  thin string

definitions.definition: Having little body fat or flesh; slim; slender; lean; gaunt.
definitions.example: thin person

definitions.definition: Of low viscosity or low specific gravity.
definitions.example: Water is thinner than honey.

definitions.definition: Scarce; not close, crowded, or numerous; not filling the space.
definitions.example: The trees of a forest are thin; the corn or grass is thin.

definitions.definition: Describing a poorly played golf shot where the ball is struck by the bottom part of the club head. See fat, shank, toe.
Lacking body or volume; small; feeble; not full.
Slight; small; slender; flimsy; superficial; inadequate; not sufficient for a covering.
definitions.example: a thin disguise

definitions.definition: Not thickly or closely; in a scattered state.
definitions.example: seed sown thin
